prompt: Set up the evaluation pipeline for our CSNL RAG tutor. I have a Zotero collection with 50 papers across VWM, attention, fMRI methods, neural coding, and psychophysics. I need: (1) a test suite of 30 queries with ground truth, (2) retrieval metrics (precision@5, recall@10, MRR), (3) generation factuality scoring. The pipeline uses PostgreSQL+pgvector with BGE-M3 1024d embeddings. Give me the complete evaluation setup.
expected_output_criteria: Creates bootstrap_ground_truth.py with 30 query templates covering 5+ domains
Each query has relevant_papers, relevant_sections, difficulty, domain fields
Retrieval eval code connects to PostgreSQL+pgvector (not ChromaDB/LanceDB)
Uses cosine similarity with 1024d vectors (BGE-M3 compatible)
Generation eval uses LLM-as-judge with explicit scoring rubric
Reports precision@5, recall@10, MRR, nDCG@10
Includes honest caveat about LLM-as-judge noise (~0.7 human agreement)
Does NOT promise DSPy auto-optimization (marks as future work)

prompt: I ran the eval and precision@5 dropped from 0.72 to 0.45 after I changed the chunk size from 512 to 1024 tokens. Help me diagnose why and set up an A/B comparison report. Show the results as an HTML report.
expected_output_criteria: Diagnoses chunk size impact on retrieval precision systematically
Sets up A/B comparison between 512-token and 1024-token chunking
Generates HTML report with side-by-side metric comparison
Includes per-query breakdown showing which queries degraded most
Suggests specific remediation: overlap increase, hybrid search tuning
Uses generate_recommendations pattern from SKILL.md
Does NOT suggest automated DSPy fix (human-in-the-loop recommendation)

prompt: Create the ground truth dataset for our 17 CRMB textbook chapters. I need queries that test understanding of each chapter topic, with relevant source sections identified. The chapters cover: visual perception, attention, working memory, long-term memory, decision making, motor control, language, emotion, consciousness, development, plasticity, computational models, neuroimaging methods, psychophysics, neural coding, population coding, and Bayesian brain.
expected_output_criteria: Produces at least 30 queries (aim for 2 per chapter = 34)
Covers all 17 chapters explicitly
Each query has: query_en, query_ko, relevant_papers, relevant_sections, difficulty, domain
Difficulty distribution: ~10 undergrad, ~12 master, ~8 PhD
Bilingual queries (English + Korean)
Includes domain-specific terminology (not generic questions)
Warns about manual verification requirement
Saves as structured JSON compatible with eval_retrieval function
